_meta.source_primary: Paul Tannery, Diophanti Alexandrini Opera Omnia, Vol.1, Teubner 1893 (introduction)
_meta.source_secondary: T.L. Heath, Diophantus of Alexandria, 2nd ed., Cambridge 1910, pp. 32–76
_meta.source_tertiary: Fabio Acerbi, Byzantine Recensions survey, HAL hal-03596963
_meta.encoding_status: partial — compound ligatures lack single Unicode codepoints; TEI scheme defined below
_meta.encoding_gap: The subtraction sign has no Unicode codepoint. Compound power symbols (ΔΥ, ΚΥ, etc.) are multi-character approximations only.

unknown_symbol.ms_form: resembles a final sigma ς or stigma ϛ; Heath: 'the symbol used for the unknown quantity in the MSS. is somewhat like the final sigma'
unknown_symbol.ambiguity_warning: ϛ (stigma) also serves as the Milesian numeral for 6. Context determines reading. In algebraic expressions, it is the unknown; in numerical lists, it is 6.

power_symbols.abbreviation_in_ms: ΔΥ (delta + upsilon, ligatured; sometimes Δ with superscript υ)
power_symbols.encoding_note: No single Unicode codepoint. The ligature ΔΥ is written as two Greek letters.

inverse_power_symbols.abbreviation_in_ms: ϛΑ (stigma + alpha with superscript or overline)

constant_symbol.abbreviation_in_ms: Μ° or Μ̄ (mu with degree mark or overline)

operational_symbols.note: Terms are simply written next to each other; positive terms precede negative

operational_symbols.symbol_in_ms: An inverted truncated ψ (Heath's description); resembles a Λ with a crossbar or an upward-pointing arrow
operational_symbols.unicode_codepoint: NONE — no Unicode codepoint exists for this symbol
operational_symbols.encoding_gap: This is the single most important unencoded symbol in Diophantus. It precedes ALL subtracted terms as a group marker, not individual terms.
operational_symbols.usage: All terms to be subtracted follow this sign as a group. E.g. 'x³ + x [minus-sign] x² + 3' means x³ + x − x² − 3

operational_symbols.symbol_in_ms: numerator written above denominator with horizontal bar; or 'ἐν μορίῳ' (in the part of) for complex fractions

milesian_numerals.description: Greek alphabetic numeral system (Milesian), used throughout. Overline (κεφαλαία) distinguishes numerals from letters.
milesian_numerals.unicode_combining_overline: U+0305 (COMBINING OVERLINE) placed after each digit character

example_expressions.diophantus_notation: ΔΥ ᾱ ϛ γ̄ Μ β̄
example_expressions.tei_encoding: <seg type="dioph:expr"><seg type="dioph:power" n="2">ΔΥ</seg> <num>ᾱ</num> <seg type="dioph:power" n="1">ϛ</seg> <num>γ̄</num> <seg type="dioph:constant">Μ</seg> <num>β̄</num></seg>

example_expressions.diophantus_notation: ΚΥ ᾱ ΔΥ γ̄ [minus] ϛ ε̄ Μ β̄
Arithmetica I.1 opening: ΔΥ ᾱ ἴσ Μ δ̄

planudes_additions.description: Maximus Planudes (c.1260–1310) added scholia in full-page width (vs. Diophantus text in two columns). The sigil σχ marks scholion openings.
planudes_additions.content: Explanatory notes on Diophantus's methods; Byzantine arithmetic context; some alternative solutions
planudes_additions.ms_identification: ff. marked with σχ in the manuscript
planudes_additions.scholarly_ref: A. Allard corrects Tannery's misidentification of the Planudes scribal hand

tei_namespace_proposal.note: Proposed namespace for encoding Diophantus notation in TEI P5. No standard exists yet — this is an open research gap.
